scope_note: QA Integer-State Pipeline Cert family [257]: certifies the two-boundary-crossing invariant of Theorem NT as a structural property of a QA-native pipeline. The observer↔QA boundary is crossed exactly twice: once at input tokenization (continuous signal → integer tuples), once at output decoding (integer tuples → continuous output). Between the two boundaries the state is pure integer tuples; there is no continuous intermediate, no re-tokenization, no float optimizer state persisting across integer-state transitions. Structurally eliminates the GLM-5 TITO misalignment failure mode (re-tokenization corrupting action↔reward alignment in async RL, arXiv:2602.15763, §4.1.2) by construction. Connects [256] QA Orbit-Resonance Attention (the middle layer this cert bounds on both ends), [159] QA Observer Core (qa_mod A1 determinism), [129] QA Projection Obstruction (layered projection framework). Source: Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-19. Reference prototype: qa_lab/qa_orbit_resonance_attention.py (input/evolve/output functions). Design doc: docs/theory/QA_GLM5_ARCHITECTURE_MAPPING.md §Failure-Mode-2. Checks ISP_1+BOUND/INT/NO_REPROJECT/DET/SRC/WIT/F; 1 PASS + 1 FAIL; self-test ok
